messages.content:
The purpose of the event is to meet Dr. bob, our new President. Alumni will also be informed of other activities that the office is preparing: rewrite this caption:
Poster:
Alumni Webinar: Meeting Our New President
May 10, 2024
6:00–7:00 PM
Zoom
Caption:
Attention all Alumni!
Don't miss this exclusive opportunity to discover new benefits and memberships now available to our Alumni! Join us for our webinar, and  meet our New President, Prof. Bob and explore the wide range of benefits and resources aimed at enhancing your success as an alumnus.
Reconnect with your fellow alumni, seize valuable opportunities, and stay connected with the network. 
Date: May 10, 2024, at 6:00 PM
Register in advance through:

Alumni Webinar: Meet Our New President and Discover New Benefits!
Join us on May 10, 2024, from 6:00-7:00 PM via Zoom for an exclusive webinar where you'll have the chance to meet our new President, Dr. Bob. Learn about the exciting activities and resources the office has in store for you, as well as how you can make the most of your alumni status. Don't miss out on this valuable opportunity to reconnect with your peers and take advantage of all the benefits available to you.
Register now to secure your spot!
in another way

Don't miss your chance to meet our new President, Dr. Bob, and discover the latest alumni benefits and activities! Join us for a special webinar on May 10, 2024, from 6:00-7:00 PM via Zoom. Reconnect with fellow alumni, explore new opportunities, and learn how to enhance your success post-graduation. Register now to reserve your spot and stay connected with our alumni network!

rearrange these points in a logical manner: Our new MUBS President
⦁	PhD in Molecular Neuroscience from Ohio State University.
⦁	Master’s degree in Education Policy, Organization, and Leadership Studies from Stanford University.
⦁	• Post-doctoral fellow at St. Jude Children's Research Hospital.
⦁	• Published researcher with over 1000 citations.
⦁	• Research interests: molecular neuroscience, education, global health.
⦁	• Founder of the Virtual Exchange Program (VEP) and the Center of Excellence in Research, Education, and Cultural Studies (CEREC).

Introducing our new MUBS President:

- PhD in Molecular Neuroscience from Ohio State University.
- Master’s degree in Education Policy, Organization, and Leadership Studies from Stanford University.
- Post-doctoral fellow at St. Jude Children's Research Hospital.
- Published researcher with over 1000 citations.
- Research interests include molecular neuroscience, education, and global health.
- Founder of the Virtual Exchange Program (VEP) and the Center of Excellence in Research, Education, and Cultural Studies (CEREC).

incorporate these sentences into a longer one: Panel 1: Bridging the Gap & Overcoming Challenges 🤝
Join us for a dynamic discussion on overcoming educational challenges. Let's shape the future of education!
Join us for Panel 1: Bridging the Gap & Overcoming Challenges 🤝 where we will have a dynamic discussion on overcoming educational challenges and collectively shape the future of education.

do the same for each of these posts: Second post:

Panel 2: Educating for a Sustainable Future 🌿
Explore the path to a sustainable future with us. Join the conversation on sustainable education!

Third post:

Panel 3: Incorporating VR & AI Responsibly in Educational Systems
Discover the responsible integration of VR & AI in education. Join us for insights on the future of learning!

Fourth post:

Panel 4: Higher Education's Role in Public Health: Navigating Challenges and Promoting Awareness
Explore the vital link between higher education and public health. Join us to navigate challenges and promote awareness!

Join us for Panel 2: Educating for a Sustainable Future 🌿 as we explore the path to a sustainable future and engage in a conversation on sustainable education.

Third post:

Discover the responsible integration of VR & AI in education by joining us for Panel 3: Incorporating VR & AI Responsibly in Educational Systems. Gain insights on the future of learning!

Fourth post:

Explore the vital link between higher education and public health by joining us for Panel 4: Higher Education's Role in Public Health. Let's navigate challenges and promote awareness together!

rewrite this caption: MUBS proudly presents its National Education Platform Conference, "Bridging Horizons, Shaping Futures," on Friday, April 26, 2024, hosted at the Ministry of Education and Higher Education. This conference will feature a series of captivating panel discussions that delve into the future of education.

Join us for Panel 1: Bridging the Gap & Overcoming Challenges 🤝 where we will have a dynamic discussion on overcoming educational challenges and collectively shape the future of education.

Join us for MUBS' National Education Platform Conference, "Bridging Horizons, Shaping Futures," on Friday, April 26, 2024, hosted at the Ministry of Education and Higher Education. This exciting conference will showcase a series of engaging panel discussions exploring the future of education. Don't miss Panel 1: Bridging the Gap & Overcoming Challenges 🤝, where we will engage in a dynamic discussion on overcoming educational challenges and shaping the future of education together.
make it shorter

Join us for MUBS' "Bridging Horizons, Shaping Futures" Conference on April 26, 2024, at the Ministry of Education. Delve into the future of education with captivating panel discussions. Don't miss Panel 1: Bridging the Gap & Overcoming Challenges 🤝 for a dynamic conversation on shaping education's future.
replace join us in panel 1

Attend Panel 1: Bridging the Gap & Overcoming Challenges 🤝 for a dynamic conversation on shaping education's future at MUBS' "Bridging Horizons, Shaping Futures" Conference on April 26, 2024, at the Ministry of Education. Delve into the future of education with captivating panel discussions.
panel 2 i mean

Attend Panel 1: Bridging the Gap & Overcoming Challenges 🤝 and Panel 2: Educating for a Sustainable Future 🌿 for captivating discussions on shaping the future of education at MUBS' "Bridging Horizons, Shaping Futures" Conference on April 26, 2024, at the Ministry of Education. Explore the path to a sustainable future and engage in dynamic conversations with industry experts.

MUBS & NWN Training: Enhancing School Skills

rewrite this article: The School of Health Sciences (SHS) at the Modern University for Business and Science (MUBS) recently organized a Health Day at the Lebanese Modern College (LMC) in Hasbaya as part of the National Education Platform (NEP) initiative. The event, which took place on April 4, aimed to promote community health and collaboration by providing valuable healthcare services to the students, families, and staff of LMC.

The Health Day, which included an Optometry Campaign, was a joint effort between MUBS and the National Wellness Network (NWN). Attendees were offered free examinations and exclusive discounts at the Hasbaya Health Center of NWN, ensuring that healthcare services were accessible to all.

The day began with informative lectures and eye-checks for students, families, and staff. Dr. Firas Azzam, Coordinator at NWN, highlighted the organization's role and commitment to community well-being. Ms. Maysaloun Raiden, Admission Officer at MUBS, shared insights about the institution's majors and achievements, aiming to inspire and inform attendees about educational opportunities at MUBS. Mrs. Sylvana Abu Ibrahim, Optometrist at NWN, delivered a captivating lecture on the importance of eye care in the digital age, addressing concerns and providing practical advice on maintaining good eye health.

The Health Day at LMC showcased MUBS's and NWN's dedication to improving the health and well-being of the LMC community, fostering a healthier and more informed environment.

The Modern University for Business and Science's School of Health Sciences (SHS) recently held a Health Day at the Lebanese Modern College (LMC) in Hasbaya as part of the National Education Platform (NEP) initiative. Taking place on April 4, the event aimed to enhance community health and collaboration by offering valuable healthcare services to LMC students, families, and staff.

Organized in partnership with the National Wellness Network (NWN), the Health Day featured an Optometry Campaign where attendees received free examinations and exclusive discounts at the Hasbaya Health Center of NWN, ensuring equitable access to healthcare services.

The event kicked off with informative lectures and eye-checks for students, families, and staff. Dr. Firas Azzam, the NWN Coordinator, emphasized the organization's commitment to community well-being, while Ms. Maysaloun Raiden, MUBS's Admission Officer, provided insights into the institution's majors and accomplishments, aiming to inspire attendees about educational opportunities at MUBS. Additionally, Mrs. Sylvana Abu Ibrahim, an Optometrist at NWN, delivered a compelling lecture on eye care in the digital age, addressing concerns and offering practical advice on maintaining optimal eye health.

Through the Health Day at LMC, MUBS and NWN showcased their dedication to enhancing the health and well-being of the LMC community, fostering a healthier and more informed environment.

I am approximately in my mid-40s, about 170 cm tall, with black hair, and I am wearing formal attire.

Me: Thank you, can you help me?
Employee: You're welcome.
Me: I want to ask you about the Bissell vacuum cleaners, what's the difference between this one and that one?
Employee: Sure, I will explain them all to you.
Me: I want to know the difference between these two.
Employee: This vacuum is for everything - carpets, floors, sofas.
Me: Okay, what about this one?
Employee: This one is for floors, it vacuums and washes hard floors. This is only for sofas.
Me: Are there any discounts on them now? 
Employee: With the complete package or just for the floor? 
Me: Just for the floor, for vacuuming and cleaning.
Employee: It has an offer for 999.
Me: Does it come with a warranty?
Employee: It has Extra warranty, meaning any problem can be fixed for free if it's a company fault. If it can't be repaired, they will give you a new one. And there's a two-year warranty from the dealer themselves.

Employee: There is also a Hoover for sofas; this one is more powerful than the Bissell in watts, this one is 400 and Bissell is 330.
Me: Alright, thank you.
Employee: You're welcome.

Conversation 2 (Saudi employee, wearing formal attire and a veil, approximately 158 cm tall):

The employee came directly without me asking for assistance.

Employee: How can I help you?
Me: I want to check the dishwashers if you can tell me more about the best one.
Employee: Bosch is the German one.
Me: What about Toshiba?
Employee: Toshiba is Polish assembly in China.
Me: Okay, are there any with three drawers available?
Employee: Yes, we can order it for you; its price is two thousand something.
Me: How many sprayers does each one have?
Employee: Three, three, all of them have three.
This is the price before and after the discount, and if you have a Good Card, you get additional discounts.
Me: How long are these offers available for?
Employee: These are the last two days.

I ask about an LG dishwasher in different colors and different prices. 
Me: What's the difference between these two dishwashers? 
Employee: Both have 10 steam programs, just different colors.
Me: Can I order it online for the same price? 
Employee: Yes, it's fine, but it's better from the branch for maintenance; they can come here immediately.
Me: Okay, thank you.

Employee 3 - Fares B.

I look at the chargers.
The employee told me they are all original Apple chargers and cost 99 with a two-year warranty from Apple.
Me: What about Samsung chargers?
Employee: You have to go to Samsung to see; it has to be from Samsung itself.
rewrite them in a 1st person paragraph in a narrative way. Not in dialogue format

As a middle-aged individual of around 40 with a height of approximately 170 cm, I found myself in formal attire while seeking assistance at a retail store. Approaching an employee, I inquired about the differences between two Bissell vacuum cleaners, wanting to understand their specific functionalities. The employee explained that one model was versatile, suitable for various surfaces like carpets, floors, and sofas, while the other was more tailored for floor cleaning and maintenance. Delving further into the details, I discovered that special offers were available, with one vacuum priced at 999 with additional warranty benefits. In addition, the employee introduced me to a Hoover model for sofas and elaborated on its enhanced power compared to the Bissell vacuum. Expressing gratitude for the information, I navigated through the store, exploring options for practical household appliances.

In another instance, a female employee dressed in formal attire, including a veil and standing at approximately 158 cm, approached me without prompting to offer assistance. I sought her insights on dishwashers, particularly inquiring about the features of Bosch and Toshiba models. Learning about the differences in their manufacturing origins and specifications, I found the employee to be knowledgeable and helpful in guiding me through the selection process. As we discussed various dishwasher options and their functionalities, including the number of drawers and sprayers, I gained a better understanding of the available choices. The employee highlighted ongoing discount offers and additional benefits with specific loyalty cards, ensuring that all essential information was provided to make an informed decision. Acknowledging the timely nature of the promotions, I absorbed the details and appreciated the employee's assistance in navigating the appliance selection process.

Shifting to a different encounter, I engaged with another employee named Fares B. while browsing through chargers. Fares informed me about the range of original Apple chargers available, emphasizing their quality and pricing, along with the accompanying warranty details. Intrigued by the prospect of Samsung chargers, I sought further clarification on their availability and authenticity, learning that Samsung-branded chargers would need to be sourced directly from Samsung for optimal compatibility and performance. Grateful for the employee's insights, I continued my exploration of technological accessories, equipped with valuable information to inform my purchasing decisions.
rewrite it in a simple language, not in a pretentious manner

I went to a store wearing nice clothes and asked the store worker about the differences between two vacuum cleaners. One was good for carpets, floors, and sofas, while the other was mainly for floors. The worker told me one cost 999 and had extra warranty. They also showed me a better Hoover vacuum for sofas. The worker was nice and helpful. Later, a lady worker in a veil helped me with dishwashers. She told me about Bosch and Toshiba dishwashers. I asked about the drawers and sprayers in the dishwashers. She said they were on sale for two more days. Then, another worker named Fares told me about Apple chargers. He said they were good and cost 99 with a warranty. When I asked about Samsung chargers, he said Samsung chargers had to be bought directly from Samsung. They were all friendly and helped me understand the products better.
mention all the details, do not summarize it

I went to a store wearing formal attire and spoke with an employee about two Bissell vacuum cleaners. One was suitable for cleaning carpets, floors, and sofas, while the other was specifically designed for floor cleaning. The employee explained that the versatile model cost 999 and came with extra warranty benefits. Additionally, they introduced a Hoover vacuum cleaner for sofas, which was more powerful than the Bissell model. Afterwards, a female employee, dressed in formal attire with a veil and around 158 cm tall, approached me without me asking for help. She provided information about Bosch and Toshiba dishwashers, including details about the number of drawers and sprayers in each model. I learned that the dishwashers were on sale for the last two days. Later, I spoke with another employee named Fares about Apple chargers, which cost 99 and came with a two-year warranty. When inquiring about Samsung chargers, Fares mentioned that they needed to be purchased directly from Samsung for authenticity. Overall, all the employees were friendly and helpful, guiding me through the various products and options available in the store.

Only one employee was available at the customer service and payment counter. The employee, dressed in a thobe and shemagh, wearing a face mask, with a height of 160 cm, dark skin, and Saudi nationality, greeted me when I approached. I inquired about the conditions for exchanging and returning a printer if purchased. The employee explained that a 30% deduction would apply if the printer was opened or used with ink, preventing it from being returned or exchanged. Seeking information about their working hours for the day, I asked the employee, who indicated they would be available until 11:00. Thanking the employee, I left with a pleasant exchange, expressing my appreciation for their assistance.

The employee showed me the Lenovo laptop suitable for school use, with 512 GB storage, 8 GB RAM, and a robust build, priced at 2000 SAR. Although it was slightly expensive, he assured me of its durability and performance. When I asked for a cheaper option, the undercover shopper recommended the i3 for 1400 SAR, mentioning its shorter lifespan limit of three years compared to the Lenovo. Recognizing the value in investing in quality, I decided to purchase the Lenovo for its endurance and robust capabilities, thanking the employee for the advice.

In the TV section, a Saudi employee with white skin and a height of about 165 cm assisted me as I inquired about a suitable screen for Sony and movies. They recommended Samsung, LG, and TCL TVs, asking about my preferred size, frequency, and specifications. Opting for a 50-inch screen with a 60 Hz refresh rate, I settled on the TCL TV model for its exceptional color display, surround sound, and warranty coverage, priced at 1600 SAR.

Informed about a TCL TV offer for 2299 SAR, I continued to browse for potential purchases, contemplating my options before conveying my farewell greetings to the undercover shopper.

rewrite this;7: I asked customer service about the Jawd card, how to activate it, and what types it is. The employee told me that there are two types of Jawd card, the gold one, the blue one, for 199 riyals, and the gold one, for approximately 590 riyals. Of course, each has advantages. The blue one approximately receives a 5% discount, and the gold one receives an approximately 15% discount. %

I inquired with customer service about the Jawd card, including how to activate it and its variations. The employee explained that there are two types of Jawd cards - the gold and blue versions priced at 199 SAR and around 590 SAR, respectively. Each card offers specific benefits; the blue card offers a 5% discount, while the gold card offers a 15% discount.

rewrite this in english:I entered the printer section and within 1 minute an employee named Shadid approached me and offered her help. 



سالت عن طابعه ليزر فقالت الموظفه هل

 تريدها ملونه ام اسود
المتسوق الخفي لا احتاجها ملون

الموظفه طيب تمام عندك هذه كانون ملونه وفيها شو اسمه سكينر اسود وابيض وملون كمان ولكن سعرها شويه غالي يجي ب 1699 دقائق اشوف لك لو عليه عرض
المسوق الخفي تمام

الموظفه ما عليها عرض ولكن بشوف لك الثانيه بدون سكينر هذه ب 1200 بدون عرض ولكن ملونه كمان ليزر
المتسوق الخفي طيب تمام في نوع ثاني

الموظفه استخدامك كثير او قليل

المتسوق الخفي والله استخدامي في اليوم 100 او الى 150 ورقه استخدمها للمدرسه والعمل اشتغل عليها يعني

الموظفه عندك هذه الطابعه الاتش بي هذه تطبع الى 6000 او 4000 ورقم ملونه و 6000 او 4000 الورقه ابيض واسود وجده ممتازه ولكن حبرها سائل اذا كان استخدامك يومي وشكن يعني ممتاز يكون جدا مناسب لك

I entered the printer section, and within a minute, an employee named Shadid approached and offered assistance. I asked about a laser printer, specifying that I did not need a colored one. The employee showed me a Canon colored printer with a scanner for 1699 SAR and mentioned she would check if there was an offer available. She then suggested another colored laser printer without a scanner priced at 1200 SAR and indicated that she would check for other options. The employee asked about my printing usage, to which I responded that I print around 100 to 150 pages daily for school and work. She then recommended an HP printer that can handle 4000 to 6000 pages, both colored and black and white, highlighting its quality but mentioning that the ink is liquid-based, suitable for frequent daily use.

I went to the air fryers section, and right as I entered, an employee approached and greeted me. I expressed my need for an air fryer with a good price and quality. The employee recommended a Philips air fryer with touch control for 399 SAR, or a Philips model without touch control for regular use priced at 350 SAR. He suggested opting for the touch control version for added convenience as it allows for easy temperature adjustment and simplifies the cooking process. Additionally, he mentioned that there was an offer on a regular control air fryer for 340 SAR and an automatic control option for 370 or 380 SAR, all of which were suitable for excellent usage, particularly the four-liter capacity for young individuals, ensuring they would suffice.

when i arrived, there was no one at the return counter i asked them a staff member named "Mohammed"  came and told me that "Abdullah" was away for a bit to go to the mosque he then offered me a drink i accepted and got my drink and they told me to wait for 10-15 minutes i sat down at the restaurant area everything was clean and sanitized i waited for a while there was someone after me and they also waited another coworker approached me asking if i wanted to refund or exchange my item i answered i wanted to refund it and they told me to wait for a bit until the staff member "Abdullah" arrives and then waited for around 20-25 minutes but the person before me went first and didn't finish quickly he was talking for a while and i waited for another 20 or so minutes then my turn came and everything went smoothly he asked if i wanted to exchange or return i answered with return he asked why i wanted to return the item i said i wasn't satisfied with the product then he proceeded to refund my money with cash since i paid with cash then thanked me for visiting and i left the building.

Upon my arrival, there was no one at the return counter. I approached a staff member named "Mohammed," who explained that "Abdullah" was temporarily away at the mosque. Mohammed kindly offered me a drink, which I accepted, and informed me to wait for 10-15 minutes. I sat in the clean and sanitized restaurant area, where I waited along with another customer who arrived after me. After a while, another coworker approached me to inquire if I wanted to refund or exchange my item. Opting for a refund, I was asked to wait for "Abdullah" to return. After waiting for around 20-25 minutes, I noticed the customer before me was attended to first and engaged in a longer conversation. Eventually, when it was my turn, the process went smoothly. I expressed my dissatisfaction with the product to Abdullah, who promptly processed my refund in cash, considering my initial payment method. I was thanked for my visit, and I left the building.

When I entered the market hall and found the clock/alarm clock section, I awaited assistance. Amin quickly noticed me and approached, offering his help. He recommended a stylish clock and inquired if I required batteries. Amin mentioned that rechargeable batteries were available but slightly pricey, suggesting that any battery from outside would be a more budget-friendly option. I expressed gratitude to Amin for his assistance and continued with my visit.

i was waiting in line behind two costumers and they finished immediately and was my turn she greeted me and asked if that was everything i was buying and asked if i had the ikea membership i answered no then she asked if i was paying via cash or card i gave her the amount needed and proceeded to give me the change almost immediately thanked me and i left.

I waited in line behind two customers who quickly finished their transactions. When it was my turn, the cashier greeted me and confirmed if the items I had were all I was purchasing. She asked if I had an IKEA membership, to which I replied no. Then, she inquired whether I was paying with cash or card. After providing the necessary payment, she promptly returned my change, thanked me, and I left the store.

Good evening, Dr. Nael. I hope all is well. I apologize for reaching out to you knowing how busy you are. I am kindly seeking an update on my decision to become a full-time employee. With the new month starting next week, I believe it would be best to contact HR this week if I wish to change my contract. I am unsure whether I should inform HR myself or if you will handle this decision. Please advise on the appropriate course of action in this matter. Thank you.

the cashier's name was Hussin. . i told him i want to return the item, he asked me the reason and said that did i change my mind, he then offered me a seat to sit and then took the card which i payed through. he apologized 2 times for me waiting for the procedure to complete. he also asked me if i was a ikea family member, he gave me his card to scan the QR code for the ikea family membership. he explained me the procedure of how to apply to this membership. he was a polite and well mannered and friendly man,

At 8:31 PM, I returned to the store to process a refund. The cashier, Hussin, assisted me with the return. I informed him of my decision to return the item, and he inquired about the reason, asking if I had a change of mind. Hussin offered me a seat, retrieved the card used for payment, and apologized twice for any wait during the process. He also inquired about my IKEA Family membership and provided his card for scanning the QR code to join. Hussin explained the membership procedure and displayed politeness and friendliness throughout the interaction.
